patterns.name: Hardcoded Password Assignment
patterns.description: Variable named 'password' (or variant) assigned a string literal
patterns.risk: Hardcoded password in source code. Risk depends on what it authenticates.
patterns.fix: Use an environment variable or secret manager.
false_positive_hints: Ignore if value is clearly placeholder: 'password123', 'changeme', 'your-password-here'
Ignore in test files using known test credentials
Ignore password validation regexes or schemas

patterns.name: Hardcoded Secret Assignment
patterns.description: Variable named 'secret' (or variant) assigned a long string literal
patterns.risk: Likely an API secret or signing key. Risk depends on the service.
patterns.fix: Use an environment variable named after the service (e.g., STRIPE_SECRET_KEY).

patterns.name: Hardcoded API Key Assignment
patterns.description: Variable named 'api_key' (or variant) assigned a long string literal
patterns.risk: Likely a real API key. Risk depends on the service.
patterns.fix: Identify the service and use the appropriate environment variable.

patterns.name: Hardcoded Token Assignment
patterns.description: Variable named 'token' (or variant) assigned a long string literal
patterns.risk: Likely an authentication token. Risk depends on the service and scope.
patterns.fix: Use an environment variable. Tokens should be obtained at runtime via OAuth/OIDC.

patterns.name: JWT Signing Secret
patterns.description: JWT signing secret hardcoded in source code
patterns.risk: Attacker can forge valid JWT tokens and bypass authentication entirely.
patterns.fix: Use JWT_SECRET environment variable. Use RS256 with public/private key pair for production.

patterns.name: URL with Basic Auth Credentials
patterns.risk: Credentials visible in source code, logs, and browser history.
patterns.fix: Separate credentials from URLs. Pass via headers or environment variables.

patterns.name: Inline Bearer Token
patterns.description: Hardcoded Bearer token in an authorization header
patterns.risk: Direct API access with the embedded credentials.
patterns.fix: Obtain tokens at runtime. Never hardcode auth headers.

patterns.name: Hardcoded Encryption Key
patterns.description: Encryption key hardcoded in source code
patterns.risk: All encrypted data can be decrypted. Defeats the purpose of encryption entirely.
patterns.fix: Use a key management service (AWS KMS, GCP KMS, Azure Key Vault, HashiCorp Vault).

patterns.name: Private/Internal IP Address
patterns.description: Private/internal IP address that may reveal network topology
patterns.risk: Reveals internal network structure. Useful for attackers doing reconnaissance.
patterns.fix: Use DNS names or configuration variables instead of hardcoded IPs.
false_positive_hints: Common in Docker, localhost development, and test configs
